summary: Revisions to "Issues and Help" pages of drivers documentation
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Example: https://mongodb.github.io/mongo-csharp-driver/2.2/issues_help/
1) doesn't include links to support team (support.mongodb.com)
2) link to community forum is no longer accurate (it's now community.mongodb.com)
3) we could include a link to file a feature request (feedback.mongodb.com)

We should go through all drivers and make sure any help/assistance pages have the correct links/directions.


    See DRIVERS-1180 for updated details.

summary: Aggregation with $out not always sent to primary
Add instructions on how to authenticate yourself in the database in the main README file, at least make the reference to it
description:
In the company, we're introducing Mongodb. 

Our is tech stack is mostly gRPC services running on Kubernetes. So we decided to build an Event Sourcing system to track down all our transactions across the microservices interactions. 

We decided to store the events into a Mongodb. At the beginning we got stuck trying to perform operations on the tables, because we need authenticate before that. It took a while to figure it out. A colleague and I got stuck for hours (working in parallel). We realized we had the same problem in the next stand up meeting. All the team agreed that some sort of example and reference to the actual Authentication documentations might come in handy. 

I found [this|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/55127143/how-to-connect-to-a-mlab-mongodb-database-in-golang] on stackoverflow and the accepted solution did the trick.

summary: Audit missing replaceErrors calls
description: Some functions (e.g. IndexView.CreateOne/CreateMany) are missing replaceErrors calls, which causes them to return error types from the "x" package. We should audit all of these to make sure the errors are translated. This will also be necessary for client-side operations timeout as that project will be adding logic to processError to correctly transform timeout errors.

summary: For nil options, InsertMany and InsertOne have different behaviors
Mark mtest package as internal and unstable
description: The mongo/integration/mtest package holds the testing framework we use for our high-level integration tests. The package API is unstable because we make changes to it when we need to support new features in spec/prose tests. At the very least, this package should have a doc.go file marking it as unstable like the x package does. We can also consider moving it to be a sub-package of internal so it's not usable outside of the driver.

summary: BSON Key conflict in unmarshaling
Drivers must raise an error if response messageLength > ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes
Return a status from ProcessError
description: For some users of the unstable driver APIs, it'd be useful to have ProcessError return a status to indicate what it actually did. This can be an enum with values like ClearedPool and MarkedUnknown. If there are cases where it could potentially do multiple things, we could return a simple struct with boolean fields instead.

summary: Extended JSON can fail to parse if undefined document field contains a document or an array
Remove SetBucketSize from TestIndexView/create_one/all_options
description: Having this option in this test will fail on latest, as the latest version doesn't support it.

summary: DownloadStream.Skip() does not handle multiple chunks correctly
description:
DownloadStream.Skip() does not seem to skip correctly when iterating over multiple chunks. The test cases here identify two cases:

[https://play.golang.org/p/z7RDRqU01ZA]

1. Skipping less than chunk size if the buffer offset is 0 results in a no-op.
2. When skipping results in the buffer offset being divisible by the chunk size on the last loop iteration in Skip, it resets the offset but does not fill the next buffer.

summary: Test failure because $listLocalSessions is not supported in API version 1
description: See DRIVERS-1505 for updated details.

summary: Versioned MongoDB API for Drivers
Add more spec tests for invalid relaxed UUIDs
description: See DRIVERS-1526 for updated details.

summary: Syntactic delights mongo/doc.go improvements
description:
- Use only mongo.Connect and not NewClient
 - Add cursor.All example

summary: Fix failing versioned API tests in new tasks
description:
There are three tests that fail on and off in the new versioned API tasks. Unsure why they didn't come up before...

There's also occasionally a seemingly random timeout in the CSE tests when apiVersion fields are sent to the server. This might have to do with an incompatibility with mongocryptd, but needs to be investigated.

summary: primitive.Decimal128 does not implement MarshalJSON or UnmarshalJSON
description: I noticed that primitive.Decimal128 were not being rendered to JSON and I think it is because the JSON MarshalJSON and UnmarshalJSON methods are not implemented for it.

summary: Remove data/*.json and use data/bson-corpus/*.json
description: As noted in the PR for GODRIVER-1746 ([in this comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/pull/560/files#r556782757]) the {{data/bson-corpus}} files appear unused. The bson-corpus tests read from {{data}}. Let's remove {{data/*.json}} and instead use {{data/bson-corpus}}. This is consistent with other spec tests, and it is the directory updated by the {{etc/update-spec-tests.sh}} script.

summary: Support parsing $uuid as extended JSON representation for subtype 4 binary
description: See DRIVERS-848 for updated details.

summary: Find `Limit` Options not work in MongoDB 2.6
mongo client has i/o timeout after network exception
description:
Hi,

I have a micro-service based on mongo client.

All is well, however, after some days, A query operations Find(xxx) failed with errors as below:

connection(192.168.20.194:3717[-123425]) incomplete read of message header: read tcp 10.233.26.252:47192->192.168.20.194:3717: use of closed network connection

and the following query operations, such as a lot of Operations Find(xxx),  failed with errors like these:

context deadline exceeded

there is no one connection between my-server and mongo-server when I type command `netstat -apn`.

Then I tried to capture tcp package by tcpdump.  also no one tcp package occured.

summary: Support PKCS8 encrypted client private keys
description:
Go's support for encrypted private keys only handles decryption of the outer PEM layer, it does not support decryption of the PEM-decoded PKCS8 data. See [Go issue#8860|https://github.com/golang/go/issues/8860].

That ticket references a 3rd party library, https://github.com/youmark/pkcs8 , which does PKCS8 decryption -- but only for a single encryption algorithm, which might be a useful starting point for full support.

summary: ErrWaitQueueTimeout should wrap context error
description:
When a request to acquire a semaphore in a connection pool returns an error, we return ErrWaitQueueTimeout [here|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/751a919b969ca11cee98f3ac49b8eac0408167eb/x/mongo/driver/topology/pool.go#L346]. However, this would only happen if the context has timed out or been cancelled, so the final user-facing error should wrap ctx.Err(). This would allow the user to do errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) and also allow our mongo.IsTimeout() function to detect this case.

We can't do this while also keeping the sentinel ErrWaitQueueTimeout value, but this is private API and users are likely not checking for it directly, so I propose we remove that error and instead introduce a new error type that wraps ctx.Err(), has an Unwrap method, and keeps the same message as ErrWaitQueueTimeout in case users are checking for substrings.

summary: Error when maps with multiple keys are used for ordered parameters
description: Add errors for using any map with more than one key as a hint option, sort option, or for index creation.

summary: add docs.go for event package
Deprecate unnecessary FindOne options
description: Our FindOneOptions type allows specifying options like BatchSize and CursorType. These aren't valid for a FindOne operation because there is no cursor created, so they can be deprecated.

summary: Have bsoncore.Array marshal into an array type instead of an embedded document
Sharing a MongoClient for metadata lookup can lead to deadlock in drivers using automatic encryption
description: See DRIVERS-1459 for updated details.

summary: Investigate failures on test-standalone-versioned-api
description:
As of adding a CSFLE internal client ([GODRIVER-1832|https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/GODRIVER-1832]), many tests in the test-standalone-versioned-api have started failing intermittently due to connection timeouts.

Investigate!

summary: Change estimatedDocumentCount() to use the $collStats Agg Stage Instead of Count Command
Improve bulkwrite batch test performance
description: TestCollection/bulk_write/insert_and_delete_with_batches is a very slow test due to how many documents it needs to have multiple batches.

summary: Failure during CommitTransaction still moves state to Committed
description:
During a transaction, if a CommitTransaction times out, the client session state is still moved to "Committed". 

This is inaccurate, as the operations within the commit will not have actually reached the server, so the transaction is not "Committed". Furthermore, if users attempt to abort the transaction when the CommitTransaction fails, they will encounter a "cannot call abortTransaction after calling commitTransaction" error, which is likely an unexpected error.

The issue is likely [here|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/mongo/session.go#L309], where we set session state to "Committed" without checking for potential errors from operation execution.

summary: Add nil client check when verifying transaction status in (Operation).Execute
description:
If an Operation is constructed with a nil client and then executed, it's possible to panic in a couple spots when verifying if the Operation is in a transaction:
 * For a WriteCommandError [here|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/5e7155c724895cbc23992b3e61a91521efbec2f6/x/mongo/driver/operation.go#L390]
 * For a normal Error [here|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/5e7155c724895cbc23992b3e61a91521efbec2f6/x/mongo/driver/operation.go#L471]

summary: Test Failure - MONGODB-AWS Auth Ubuntu 18.04
description:
MONGODB-AWS Auth 4.4 Ubuntu 18.04 and MONGODB-AWS Auth latest Ubuntu 18.04 have been failing on Evergreen for a while. It's probably worth investigating why since we've made a recent push to make Evergreen green again :)

At first glance, the issue seems to be in setup on evergreen tools. It's likely this issue exists for other drivers, too.

[Failure|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_aws_auth_test__version~4.4_os_aws_auth~ubuntu1804_64_go_1_15_aws_auth_test_0b1d3d4dc9ec6dea0b473c7fcd14dfcda17ee792_21_02_08_23_03_38]

summary: Implement change stream oplog parsing code for delta oplog entries
Support AWS authentication with temporary credentials in CSFLE
description: See DRIVERS-1490 for updated details.

summary: topology.connection TLS handshake never times out
description:
With TLS configured on a toplogy, the the {{connection.connect()}} method can hang forever. From what I can tell, timeouts are applied to all operations in that method _except_ {{tls.Client.Handshake -}} if the remote server is up but mongod is hung, the {{Handshake}} method hangs indefinitely.

We discovered this bug after noticing that the driver continues to route traffic to servers that have crashed. If a mongod exits in a way that triggers a core dump (segfault, i/o error, etc.), the core dump can take a couple of minutes to write to disk - during this time, no topology updates are triggered in the driver, heartbeats hang, and server selection still returns the bad server.

This is fairly simple to repro:
* Start a mongod with SSL support
* Start this slightly modified version of the [server_monitoring example|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/x/mongo/driver/examples/server_monitoring/main.go]: https://gist.github.com/bfink13/df7a72b46ce5c21ae9888ff60a36d54e
* Send a SIGSTOP to the mongod process: {{kill -STOP <pid of mongod>}}
* Observe that topology updates stop being generated
* If the mongod process is resumed ({{kill -CONT <pid of mongod>}}) or killed, topology updates resume

summary: Fix set temp creds failures on Mac and Windows
description:
All [Windows|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_tests_nonlegacy_servers_with_snappy_support__version~3.4_os_ssl_32~windows_64_go_1_15_test_replicaset_auth_nossl_8307eb1f79a0b0375ea9fbdb19ae78d68e158ddc_21_03_05_23_05_48] and [Mac|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_tests_nonlegacy_servers_with_snappy_support__version~3.4_os_ssl_32~osx_go_1_15_test_replicaset_auth_nossl_8307eb1f79a0b0375ea9fbdb19ae78d68e158ddc_21_03_05_23_05_48] tests seem to be failing because of an error during the execution of the [set-temp-creds|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/master/.evergreen/csfle/set-temp-creds.sh] shell script.

It looks like boto3 is being installed, but the script is having trouble importing it. The issue might be that the PYTHON environment variable needs to be set to the same python where boto3 is installed.

Additionally, we're installing libmongocrypt from an outdated URL in Windows tasks. The URL should be updated to install v1.2.0, which supports authenticating with temporary credentials.

summary: Add Versioned API test for abortTransaction
Versioned API database aggregation test only works on 4.9+ servers
Update versioned api transaction-handling spec test
Drivers should not inspect error messages for "not master" or "node is recovering"
Unable to unmarshal external json with object id as hex string
panic in bsoncore StringValue function on malformed document
BSON cstrings are not properly validated
description:
BSON marshalling functions would incorrectly handle null bytes embedded in BSON key names and the pattern/options fields of a BSON regex value. BSON marshalling functions now correctly validate and error if there is an embedded null byte in BSON key names or the pattern/options fields of a BSON regex value.
h1. CVE ID: CVE-2021-20329

*Title*: Specific cstrings input may not be properly validated in the MongoDB Go Driver
 *Description*: Specific cstrings input may not be properly validated in the MongoDB Go Driver when marshalling Go objects into BSON. A malicious user could use a Go object with specific string to potentially inject additional fields into marshalled documents. This issue affects all MongoDB GO Drivers up to (and including) 1.5.0.
 *CVSS score*: 6.8
 CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N
 *Affected products and versions*, MongoDB Go Driver versions <= 1.5.0
 *Underlying operating systems affected*: All

summary: GetFailed event does not always return correct reason
description:
If the connection pool is empty and the {{connect}} call on the newly created connection fails, we publish a {{GetFailed}} CMAP event. This event's {{Reason}} field should be set to {{ReasonConnectionErrored}}.

Incorrect event construction is at [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/a62c7d7c704d9269a5399f80e266fdefb4f5da39/x/mongo/driver/topology/pool.go#L443.|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/a62c7d7c704d9269a5399f80e266fdefb4f5da39/x/mongo/driver/topology/pool.go#L443]

summary: Export extractError function
description: The extractError function in x/mongo/driver/errors.go converts a server response document into one of our low-level error types, which can then be fed directly into ProcessError to do SDAM error handling. This isn't required for correctness, but exporting this function would make it easier to do this in projects that depend on our low-level APIs.

summary: Define error handling behavior of writeErrors and writeConcernError on Mongos
Change ServerID to ServiceID
description: In the LB spec, the "serverId" field has been renamed to "serviceId". We should make a corresponding change in the driver.
